commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2da5793a6055db505015da1a5141826c3f409f56)
commands.review_summary:
The PR allows `channels.webchat`, adds channel-default chunk fields and fallback logic, extends chunk tests, and changes optimistic Web UI attachment placeholders to avoid inline data URLs.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +105. Total +125 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's validation allow-list omits `webchat`, so `channels.webchat` is rejected before runtime chunk resolution. I did not run the CLI because this review is read-only, but the validation path is direct.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs human PR review because the config/default contract and global fallback scope need maintainer approval, and automation cannot provide the external contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(webchat): allow webchat channel config and channels.defaults fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Allow `webchat` as a valid channel id in config validation so users can configure `channels.webchat.textChunkLimit` and `channels.webchat.chunkMode`
- Add `textChunkLimit` and `chunkMode` to `ChannelDefaultsConfig` type, supporting global defaults via `channels.defaults`
- Make `resolveTextChunkLimit` / `resolveChunkMode` fall back to `channels.defaults` values when no provider-specific config exists

## Fixes
- Fixes #87897 — Web UI answers truncated and `channels.webchat` config rejected as unknown channel

## Verification
- All 63 chunk tests pass
- TypeScript type check passes for modified files
- Config validation now accepts `channels.webchat` key

## Behavior addressed
Users can now configure webchat text chunk limits in two ways:
```json
{ "channels": { "webchat": { "textChunkLimit": 16000 } } }
```
or globally:
```json
{ "channels": { "defaults": { "textChunkLimit": 16000 } } }
```
target.author: xianshishan
